name: pyezvizapi
documentation: Pilot your Ezviz cameras with this module. Please view readme on github

functions.name: pyezvizapi.utils.convert_to_dict
documentation: Recursively convert a string representation of a dictionary to a dictionary.

functions.name: pyezvizapi.utils.string_to_list
documentation: Convert a string representation of a list to a list.

functions.name: pyezvizapi.utils.fetch_nested_value
documentation:
Fetch the value corresponding to the given nested keys in a dictionary.

If any of the keys in the path doesn't exist, the default value is returned.

Args:
    data (dict): The nested dictionary to search for keys.
    keys (list): A list of keys representing the path to the desired value.
    default_value (optional): The value to return if any of the keys doesn't exist.

Returns:
    The value corresponding to the nested keys or the default value.

functions.name: pyezvizapi.utils.decrypt_image
documentation:
Decrypts image data with provided password.

Args:
    input_data (bytes): Encrypted image data
    password (string): Verification code

Raises:
    PyEzvizError

Returns:
    bytes: Decrypted image data

functions.name: pyezvizapi.utils.deep_merge
documentation:
Recursively merges two dictionaries, handling lists as well.

Args:
dict1 (dict): The first dictionary.
dict2 (dict): The second dictionary.

Returns:
dict: The merged dictionary.

documentation: Ezviz cloud MQTT client for push messages.

methods.name: pyezvizapi.camera.EzvizCamera.do_not_disturb
documentation:
Enable/Disable do not disturb.

if motion triggers are normally sent to your device as a
notification, then enabling this feature stops these notification being sent.
The alarm event is still recorded in the EzViz app as normal.

Features are represented as switches. This api is for alternative switch types to turn them on or off.

All day recording is a good example.

methods.name: pyezvizapi.client.EzvizClient.set_device_feature_by_key
documentation:
Change value on device by setting the iot-feature's key.

The FEATURE key that is part of 'device info' holds
information about the device's functions (for example light_switch, brightness etc.).
